document_title: Preparing a research article or lab report with rho-class
abstract: Rho-class was designed for creating professional \LaTeX{} documents, including research articles, technical reports, and academic documentation. It enhances the writing experience through intuitive custom environments, multilingual support, and refined typography. The class integrates Fira Sans for headings and highlighted elements, Fira Mono for code listings, and STIX2 for body text, ensuring consistent styling across tables, figures, equations, listings, and captions. It is worth to mention that this template is inspired by an earlier work, the \href{https://es.overleaf.com/latex/templates/tau-class-lab-report-template/chhshmhxstsq}{tau-class}. \textit{New or updated features are marked with an asterisk (*)}.
body_sentences: This guide will help you understand the features and proper use of the template, enabling you to efficiently structure and format your documents with clarity and precision.
This class includes the following files in the rho-class folder: rho.cls — The main class file.
rhoenvs.sty — A package containing custom environments.
rhobabel.sty — A support package for multilingual.
README.md — With essential information.
Additionally, this template includes a sample document main.tex, a bibliography file rho.bib, as well as figures, tables and code examples to help you get started quickly.
License This template was developed by Guillermo Jimenez, licensed under Creative Commons CC BY 4.0.
You are free to adapt, share, and distribute it, provided that proper credit is given to the original author.
Document Styling Document Type Before the main title, a brief descriptor indicates the type of document (e.g., Lab Report, Research Article, etc).
This is controlled by the command.
If no such label is needed, omit the command and the title will automatically reposition itself to maintain proper vertical spacing and visual alignment.
Abstract and Keywords The abstract and keywords are declared in the preamble of the document with ...
environment alongside the dedicated command to allow the class file to automatically handle their formatting and positioning during the title creation process.
Lettrine The command, provides a personalized lettrine for the beginning of the first paragraph as shown in this example.
Table of Contents This class includes a customized design for the table of contents, which is disabled by default.
To enable it, insert the command before your first section.
Cross-Reference Commands* Rho-class provides shorthand commands for cross-referencing figures, tables, code listings, and equations.
To customize the label format, edit the rhobabel.sty file.
Label translations for Spanish are handled automatically.
— Uses the label style defined by .
— Uses the label style defined by .
— Uses the label style defined by .
— Uses the label style defined by .
— Uses the label style defined by .
These commands generate fully hyperlinked references, both the label and the number, ensuring navigation to the referenced element when clicked in the PDF.
Figures fig:figure shows a 3D surface visualization of a bivariate function.
[H] [width=0.7 ] figures/Example.pdf 3D surface obtained from PGFPlots PFGPlots.
fig:figure Tables tab:table shows an example table of some astronomical objects.

[H] Astronomical Object Data tab:table > p 0.25 > p 0.29 > p 0.31 Object Type Distance (Light Years) \\ Alpha Centauri Star system 4.37 \\ Betelgeuse Red supergiant star 642.5 \\ Andromeda Spiral galaxy 2.537 million \\ Earth Planet 0 \\ Sirius Binary star system 8.6 \\ Note: The table contains data of some famous celestial objects.
The command is used to add notes to tables easily.
Document Data Front-Matter Footer Block* A new block called , has been added to the bottom right of the first page, acting as a floating element which stays fixed in place.
This replaces the corresponding author block previously located above the abstract in earlier versions.
The block was created to give you greater control over supplementary document data, such as publication dates, licensing information, extended author details, and other front-matter notes.
You can fully customize its content to suit your needs.
Additionally, the new command serves as a flexible alternative to .
Since modifying the standard command can be complex and its positioning is often rigid, was introduced to offer a more adaptable solution without sacrificing functionality.
You can use || as many times as needed since it automatically ensures that each entry remains correctly associated.
For best results, I recommend using text symbols rather than mathematical ones (e.g., | |, | |, etc.).
If is not used, will automatically adjust its position.
The implementation was designed to handle any combination of front-matter elements you might need when starting your document.
In this example template, you’ll find a minimal demonstration that combines both to illustrate their interaction.
Headers and Footers* Headers On all pages except the first, the document title and journal appear in the header.
Since twoside layout is enabled, its position alternates depending on whether the page is odd or even.
Footers In the first page, six elements display supplementary information for your document: — Displays the journal name, university, institute, company, or any institutional affiliation.
— Displays the current date.
— Specifies the volume number of the publication.
— Specifies the issue number within the volume.
— Defines the full journal title.
— Shows the current page number and the total count.
In all cases, you can freely rearrange or customize the order of these elements by modifying the class file to suit your needs.
If any of them are not required, simply omit the corresponding command — the remaining elements will automatically adjust their spacing to remain evenly distributed.
Rho Custom Packages Rhobabel.sty This package have all the commands that automatically translate from English to Spanish when this custom package is defined.
By default, this document has its content in English.
However, at the beginning of the document you will find a recommendation when writing in Spanish.
You may modify this package if you want to use other language than English or Spanish.
This will make easier to translate your document without having to modify the class document.
Rhoenvs.sty This package provides custom environments designed to enhance the visual presentation and structure of your document.
Key examples include rhoenv, info, and note.
Their style is defined by rhoenvstyle — allowing you to customize their appearance according to your document’s requirements.
An example using the rhoenv environment is shown below.
[frametitle=Custom Title] This is an example of the custom title environment.
To add a title type this command |[frametitle=Custom Title]| next to the beginning of this environment (as shown in this example).
The info and note are environments which have a predefined title and translate their title to Spanish automatically when this language is defined.
Equations eq:equation shows the Schrödinger equation as an example.
eq:equation i t (r,t) = [- 2 2m 2 + V(r,t) ] (r,t) The amssymb and amsmath packages were not required, as STIX2 font incorporates mathematical symbols for writing quality equations.
If you would like to change the values that adjust the spacing above and below the equations, change the | | value until the preferred spacing is set.
The default value is set to 9pt.
Codes Coding with Minted* The minted package offers customized features for adding codes.
In addition, the template is designed to work seamlessly with Fira Code, a monospaced font specially crafted for programming environments.
If your are using a desktop app as TeXstudio, try these steps to make this package work on Windows: Install Python — A stable version (e.g., v.3.11) Open the terminal and type pip install Pygments.
Update if a newer version is available.
Go to TeXStudio settings and change the default compiler to pdflatex --shell-escape -interaction=nonstopmode \ .tex.
Compile and wait for the result.
-9pt [frametitle=Caution] Ensure that pygments is properly installed and added to your system’s PATH.
Otherwise, you may encounter compilation errors.
Additionally, enable shell escape when compiling, as it is required for minted to process and highlight code.
In Overleaf, this package is easier to use since it does not require any additional installations or modifications — just add the code as shown in the example.
lst:example shows a Python example.
python example.py Python code example with minted.
lst:example You can customize its design changing command.
The different styles offered by the minted package can be preview through this link — .

Coding with Listings Since minted requires additional installations and can be complex in some desktop \ editors, you can use the listings package instead, which provides a simpler way to include code.
[language=python] example.py lst:example2 Python code example with listings.
While listings is a simpler and widely supported package for code formatting, minted offers a more modern and powerful approach.
It leverages the Pygments syntax highlighter to deliver superior coloring, language support, and styling options.
One of its key advantages is the ability to easily switch between built-in color styles.
In contrast, listings requires manual setup for colors, fonts, and formatting rules.
For users who prefer fine-tuned customization, the styling options for listings are organized in rho.cls file and can be modified at any time to suit individual preferences.
Inline Code* As shown in this template, inline code has a custom style for improved visual appeal.
To use it, simply add command and place your code inside the braces.
Numberless section toc section Numberless section Declaring a numberless section automatically inserts a square symbol before the title.
This unique feature is specific to first-level sections within this class.
As this symbol also affects the table of contents and references titles, users who prefer to disable it may edit the section styling parameters in rho.cls.
Bibliography Bibliography management is handled by biblatex, with the default citation and reference style set to IEEE.
The citestyle=numeric-comp option is enabled, allowing multiple citations to be grouped within a single bracket (e.g., davis2023signalflow,bell2022codefont).
The citation format can be customized directly in rho.cls to suit any preferred style.
FAQ toc section FAQ How do I manage my references?
toc subsection How do I manage my references?
To manage your references, I recommend using the tool scribbr.
You can simply enter the URL or create your own citation, and then export it to using the options in the three-dot menu.
The generated citation can be copied and pasted into rho.bib, the file designated for bibliography management.
You may rename this file, but if you do, remember to update the command in rho.cls under the biblatex section.
Some platforms, such as Google Scholar or scientific journals, provide citations directly in BibTeX format.
Therefore, check if there is a ``how to cite this document'' section to streamline the citation process even further.
If you have any further questions, you can refer to the following page — Bibliography management with biblatex.
What should I do with the example files?
toc subsection What should I do with the example files?
The template includes sample content — such as an example figure, bibliography entries, and the example.py script — to demonstrate how the layout and features work.
Once you start customizing the document for your own use, feel free to delete all example files and entries you don’t need.
How do I place equations easily?
toc subsection How do I place equations easily?
For equations, we have two options: inline or on its own line.
For inline equations, simply place a dollar sign (\ ) at the beginning and end of the equation.
However, if you want the equation to be displayed on its own line, you need to use the equation environment.
If you find it challenging to write formulas directly in , you can use text editors like Word.
In the equations menu, you can select \ in the conversion section and copy and paste the equation you wrote into one of these two environments.
How do I change the paper size?
toc subsection How do I change the paper size?
By default, this class was adapted for a4paper and test it with letterpaper.

The following paper sizes are available in : letterpaper (11 8.5 in) legalpaper (14 8.5 in) executivepaper (10.5 7.25 in) a4paper (21 29.7 cm) a5paper (21 14.8 cm) b5paper (25 17.6 cm) Contact me toc section Contact me Have questions, suggestions, or an idea for a new feature?
Found a bug or working on a project you’d like to invite me to?
Feel free to reach out — I’d be happy to help, collaborate, or fix the issue.
Your feedback helps me improve my templates!
